title: File Recovery
cardText: Recover files that have been deleted, moved, lost, or become inaccessible.
Check common locations, backups, cloud storage, recycle bin, and recovery options.
Separate simple file location issues from storage failures.
text: This guide is for files that appear to be missing, deleted, or inaccessible.
Determine whether the file was deleted, moved, synchronized, or lost due to storage problems.
Use the safest recovery options first.
Avoid writing new data to a drive if accidental deletion is suspected.

identifySituation.title: Identify the problem
body: Determine whether the file was deleted, moved, renamed, or became inaccessible.
Understanding what happened helps choose the safest recovery method.

searchForFile.title: Search for the file
body: Use the operating system search feature.
Search by file name, extension, or partial file name.
The file may have been moved or renamed.

fileLocated.title: File located
body: The file was found.
Move it to a known location and verify it opens correctly.

checkRecycleBin.title: Check Recycle Bin
body: Open the Recycle Bin and search for the missing file.
Files deleted normally are often stored here until permanently removed.

restoreRecycleBin.title: Restore file
body: Restore the file from the Recycle Bin.
Verify the file opens correctly after restoration.

checkCloudStorage.title: Check cloud storage
body: Review OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox, or other cloud storage services.
The file may have been moved, synchronized, or deleted through cloud sync.

restoreCloudFile.title: Restore cloud file
body: Restore the file using the cloud provider's recovery tools or recycle bin.
Verify the recovered file opens correctly.

checkPreviousVersions.title: Check previous versions
body: Some operating systems and storage systems maintain previous versions.
Check file history, restore points, snapshots, or version history.

restorePreviousVersion.title: Restore previous version
body: Restore the required version of the file.
Verify the restored copy contains the expected data.

checkBackups.title: Check backups
body: Review local backups, external drives, NAS devices, or backup software.
Backups often provide the safest recovery method.

restoreBackup.title: Restore from backup
body: Restore the file from the backup source.
Verify the recovered file opens correctly.

checkRecentDeletion.title: Was the file recently deleted?
body: Recovery success is generally higher when deletion occurred recently.
Minimize activity on the affected drive to avoid overwriting recoverable data.

attemptRecoverySoftware.title: Attempt file recovery software
body: Use reputable recovery software on the affected drive.
Recover files to a different drive whenever possible.
Avoid installing recovery tools on the affected drive.

recoverySuccessful.title: File recovered
body: The file was successfully recovered.
Create a backup to prevent future data loss.

limitedRecovery.title: Recovery may be limited
body: Older deletions are more likely to have been overwritten.
Recovery may still be possible but success rates are lower.

checkDriveHealth.title: Check drive health
body: Determine whether the drive is showing errors, unusual noises, SMART warnings, or connection problems.
Drive faults require different handling than accidental deletion.

stopUsingDrive.title: Stop using the drive
body: Continued use can worsen data loss.
Power down the device if the data is critical.
Avoid writing new data to the affected drive.

replaceDrive.title: Replace failing drive
body: The drive appears unreliable.
Replace the drive and restore data from backups if available.

checkFilePermissions.title: Check file permissions
body: The file may exist but access may be restricted.
Verify ownership and permissions.

fixPermissions.title: Correct permissions
body: Adjust permissions or ownership as appropriate.
Verify access to the file afterwards.

professionalRecovery.title: Professional recovery recommended
body: Basic recovery methods have been exhausted.
If the data is valuable, consult a professional data recovery service.
Avoid further use of the affected drive until recovery options have been assessed.
